title: Latin Night at Sebastian Pintxos
location: Sebastian Pintxos, 818 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Friday-night Latin social in the heart of Virginia-Highland — salsa, bachata, reggaetón rotation. Pintxos and cocktails on the menu, beginner-friendly floor, the kind of low-commitment Friday where you dance, snack, and decide together whether to stay for round two.

title: Styx & Chicago — The Windy Cities Tour
location: Ameris Bank Amphitheatre, 2200 Encore Pkwy, Alpharetta, GA
description: Two '70s rock institutions on one stage — sing-along classics from 'Carry On Wayward Son' to '25 or 6 to 4'. Lawn-seat date energy: blanket, snacks, and an open-air singalong that turns into a slow dance by the encore.

title: Karlous Miller at Helium Comedy Club (Couple's Package)
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
price: Couple's Package: 2 premium seats + $90 food & beverage credit
description: 85 South / Wild 'N Out star Karlous Miller in an intimate showroom — the venue literally sells a Couple's Package with reserved seating and a $90 F&B credit. Premium, no-stress date night with a guaranteed laugh.

title: Singer Jazz McKenzie — Perfect Note Atlanta (from The Voice)
location: Perfect Note Atlanta, 1520 Federal Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Dinner-and-a-show date night with a Voice-season vocalist. Sit-down service, candles, and a real R&B set — the kind of evening where conversation stops and shoulders lean in.

title: Savanna Nights at Zoo Atlanta — Boy Bands & Girl Groups
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Cocktails, animal encounters, and a live Boy Bands & Girl Groups tribute set, all in the zoo after hours. Built-in 'first date' energy — fun, walkable, and full of moments to point and laugh at together.

title: Jimmy Buffett's Coral Reefer Band
location: Synovus Bank Amphitheater at Chastain Park, 4469 Stella Dr NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Margaritaville energy under the trees at Chastain Park. Picnic + wine on the lawn, 'Margaritaville' singalong at sunset — the platonic ideal of a summer Atlanta date.

title: Downtown Live: Jazz & Seafood — Hapeville
location: Jess Lucas Y-Teen Park, 3444 N Fulton Ave, Hapeville, GA
description: Free outdoor jazz + seafood night in Hapeville's downtown pocket park — live band, food vendors, and that small-town summer evening energy. A low-key, no-pressure Saturday date with great food and easy conversation.

title: Reggae After Dark — Free Entry + Drink Until 11 PM
location: Cafe Circa, 464 Edgewood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
price: Free entry; free drinks until 11 PM
description: Late-night reggae + dancehall vibes in the Old Fourth Ward / Edgewood corridor. Free entry and a free-drink window makes it a no-brainer Saturday nightcap — couples who want reggae energy without committing to a full amphitheater ticket.

title: Candlelight: Neo-Soul Favorites — Prince, Childish Gambino & More
location: The Chapel on Sycamore, Decatur, GA
description: A candlelit live-string tribute to Prince, Childish Gambino, Lauryn Hill, and more. Surrounded by candles, in a chapel — the kind of slow, beautiful Saturday night that turns into a long talk on the porch afterwards.

title: Somewhere Else — Photography Exhibition Opening (Origin Atlanta)
location: Origin Atlanta (a Wyndham Hotel), Atlanta, GA
description: A photography opening from Twenty7Exposures at a downtown hotel — intimate gallery setting, meet-the-artist vibes, then stay for cocktails in the lobby. A short, sweet, walk-around date in the heart of the city.

title: Lionel Richie & Earth, Wind & Fire — Sing a Song All Night Long
location: State Farm Arena, 1 State Farm Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Two Hall of Fame acts, one night of 'All Night Long' / 'September' energy. Bring a partner who likes to dance in the aisle — the whole arena becomes a wedding reception by the encore.

title: Rooftop Salsa & Bachata Social (Politan Row)
location: Politan Row at The Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Sunday-night rooftop salsa + bachata with skyline views and a food hall downstairs. Beginner-friendly floor with instructors on hand — take a lesson together, then dinner or drinks at Politan Row after.

title: Amy Sherald: American Sublime — High Museum of Art (Final Stop)
location: High Museum of Art, 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Final stop on the national tour of Georgia native Amy Sherald's most comprehensive exhibition to date. A meaningful, conversation-rich date — slow walk, big paintings, lunch in Midtown after.

title: The E.A.V. Comedy Show at El Sótano
location: El Sótano, East Atlanta Village, Atlanta, GA
description: Stand-up showcase in the heart of EAV — a low-key Wednesday night that pairs perfectly with dinner and drinks on Flat Shoals Ave. Local comics, intimate room, easy midweek reset for couples.

title: Gallery FC Celebrates DR. DAX — Group Exhibition
location: Gallery FC, Atlanta, GA
description: A celebration exhibition for Dr. Dax at Gallery FC — a smaller, conversation-friendly opening with a real community feel. Pair with dinner in the neighborhood for an easy Thursday date.

title: Afro Caribbean vs Hip Hop & R&B — Embr Lounge
location: Embr Lounge, 1136 Crescent Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Two-room DJ showdown — Afrobeats/reggae/dancehall in one room, hip-hop and R&B in the other. Late-night Midtown dance floor, couples-friendly crowd, easy to slip in for an hour or stay till last call.

title: PHYSIQUE (2026) — Juried Exhibition Opening at Kai Lin Art
location: Kai Lin Art, 309 E Paces Ferry Rd NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Kai Lin Art's first-ever juried exhibition — opening-night mixer with the artists, drinks, and a chance to actually talk to the people making the work. A Westside gallery date that ends with dinner on the BeltLine.

title: Dustin Ross at City Winery Atlanta
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Storytelling-style stand-up with a wine-paired dinner option on-site. Date-night formula solved: dinner + show in one ticket, sit-down seats, no dance floor pressure.

title: Daniel Caesar — Son of Spergy Tour
location: State Farm Arena, 1 State Farm Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Daniel Caesar in arena mode — soft R&B, gospel-adjacent vocals, and the kind of set that turns a date into a memory. Pick seats mid-floor; the room sings every chorus with him.

title: Tropical Latin Night at Sanctuary Nightclub
location: Sanctuary Nightclub, Atlanta, GA
description: Friday-night Latin social — salsa, bachata, reggaetón rotation. A couples' favorite: take a lesson together on the floor, then order a mojito and a selfie.

title: Tanda — Afro House at District Atlanta
location: District Atlanta, 1271 Glenwood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Tanda Global Sounds brings an Afro House night to District — a sleek, dance-floor-first setting for couples who want a more grown-up Afrobeats/Afro-house experience. Think deep percussion, golden lighting, and a room that pulses.

title: Noche Tropical at El Super Pan — Battery Atlanta
location: El Super Pan at Battery Atlanta, 855 Battery Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night Latin party at the Battery — salsa/bachata/reggaetón with a Latin food kitchen still open downstairs. A high-energy Friday night with dinner + dancing in one stop.

title: ANATOMY X — Christmas-in-July Sip N Paint
location: NDA Social, Atlanta, GA
description: A guided paint-and-sip night — beginners welcome, materials included, drinks in hand. A playful, no-skill-required date where the worst-case is a bad painting and the best-case is a keepsake.

title: Buju Banton & Stephen Marley: Roots and Rhymes Tour
location: Cellairis Amphitheatre at Lakewood, 2002 Lakewood Way SW, Atlanta, GA
description: A reggae double-header on the lawn — Bob Marley's son and a living legend together. Bring a blanket, bring a cooler, stay for the singalongs under the stars.

title: INNER VIEWS Art Exhibition — Opening
location: One Contemporary Gallery, 395 Edgewood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: A collective musing on home, family, and community by 14 contemporary artists across multiple mediums. Quiet, thoughtful Saturday-afternoon gallery date in Edgewood — pair with lunch on the BeltLine.

title: Beats on the Block — Park Tavern (Midtown)
location: Park Tavern, 500 10th St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: All-day outdoor electronic-music block party at Park Tavern overlooking Piedmont Park — diverse genres, food, vendors, and a sunset over the Midtown skyline. Free with RSVP, easy to drop in for an hour or stay till dark.

title: Ink Studded Vegan Fest — Lithonia
location: 1879 S Stone Mountain Lithonia Rd, Lithonia, GA
description: Plant-based food festival with vegan vendors, tattoo artists, and back-to-school energy. A niche, off-the-beaten-path date for a couple that wants a vegetarian/vegan day out beyond the typical brunch spot.

title: James 'Murr' Murray (Impractical Jokers) at Helium
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: Murr from Impractical Jokers on stage in Alpharetta — the kind of show where you'll be quoting lines to each other for weeks. Date-night + show + late dinner is the move.

title: Savanna Nights at Zoo Atlanta — Decades Night (Finale)
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: The final Savanna Nights of the summer — cocktails, animal encounters, and a Decades-night live set. Last-call romantic before the season closes; a memorable 'I can't believe we did that' kind of date.

title: UTOPIA: Old School Reggaeton Edition — District Atlanta
location: District Atlanta, 1271 Glenwood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: A throwback reggaetón takeover at District — golden-era perreo, room full of couples, late-night East Atlanta energy. A dance-first date that ends with a 3 AM slice of pizza somewhere.

title: Steel Pulse & The Original Wailers ft. Al Anderson — Reggae Against Racism Tour
location: The Eastern, 777 Memorial Dr SE Building C, Atlanta, GA
description: Two foundational reggae acts on one bill — Steel Pulse and the Original Wailers (with Al Anderson) in the Eastern's premium listening room. Sunday-night closer for couples who want big reggae history in a sit-down setting.

title: Rooftop Salsa & Bachata Social — Reverb Hotel
location: Reverb Hotel Atlanta, 89 Centennial Olympic Park Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Downtown rooftop with skyline views, a free 4:30 lesson for beginners, then social dancing. The $20 entry is a steal for a built-in date-night: take the class, dance the social, dinner in Castleberry Hill after.

title: Sun Havana Nights — Salsa, Bachata & Latin Dancing at La Industria
location: La Industria (new location), Atlanta, GA
description: A Sunday afternoon/evening salsa + bachata class-then-social at a new Latin-dance venue, with food on site. Built for couples: warm-up class, social dancing, and a sit-down bite — all in one ticket.

title: Unplugged: A Tribute to Anita Baker & Alicia Keys
location: The Betty, Buckhead, Atlanta, GA
description: Singer-songwriter tribute night honoring two queens of soulful R&B. Buckhead date-night setting — small room, two voices, candlelight, and a built-in reason to hold someone's hand during 'If I Ain't Got You.'

title: Dinner and A Date: A Night of Soul — Apache XLR
location: Apache XLR, Atlanta, GA
description: A dedicated date-night soul show — the entire event is built around couples, dining, and live R&B. Easy, on-theme, Sunday-evening slow-dance material.

title: Soulful Sunday R&B Brunch — Perfect Note Marietta
location: Perfect Note Atlanta — Marietta, 3000 Windy Hill Rd STE 116, Marietta, GA
description: A Sunday R&B brunch with a live vocalist while you eat — bottomless mimosas, sit-down service, no DJ shouting over your conversation. The slow, grown-up version of a date brunch.

title: Midsummer Market at Wild Heaven West End
location: Wild Heaven Beer: West End Brewery & Gardens, 1010 White St SW, Atlanta, GA
description: Sunday-afternoon maker's market on the West End brewery lawn — local vendors, beer garden, food trucks. A walking, browsing, sun-on-the-skin kind of date that ends with a flight of beers in the shade.

title: Midsummer Food Truck Alley — Georgia Freight Depot
location: The Georgia Freight Depot, Atlanta, GA
description: Three-day rotating food-truck alley in downtown — vegetarian and vegan-friendly options alongside the BBQ and taco trucks. A walking, sharing, deciding-together kind of weekend that ends in ice cream.

title: Back to Black: The Music of Amy Winehouse — City Winery
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: A live tribute to Amy Winehouse in City Winery's sit-down room — wine, dinner service, and a voice channeling one of the most soulful catalogs of the 2000s. Slow-dance material, end to end.

title: Jazz Affair: Dinner & A Date Jazz Night — Apache XLR
location: Apache XLR, 393 Marietta St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: A purpose-built 'Dinner & A Date' jazz night — the whole show is framed around couples at a table, slow sets, and a real sit-down menu. Easy midweek anniversary move without the anniversary pressure.

title: Tamar Braxton at City Winery Atlanta
location: City Winery Atlanta, 675 Ponce De Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Tamar Braxton's soulful, confessional R&B at City Winery's sit-down room — wine, dinner service, and a vocalist with serious range. A grown-up Thursday-night date for couples who love real singers.

title: Deante Hitchcock at Buckhead Theatre
location: Buckhead Theatre, 3110 Roswell Rd, Atlanta, GA
description: Atlanta's own Deante Hitchcock at the Buckhead Theatre — sharp, witty ATL hip-hop with a hometown crowd that knows the words. A standing-room date with energy: dinner on Roswell Rd before, late-night drinks after.

title: Soul Sessions ATL — Eddie's Attic
location: Eddie's Attic, 515 N McDonough St, Decatur, GA
description: Late-night soul showcase at Decatur's iconic listening room. Two voices, no loud bar chatter, and a real songwriter crowd — exactly the kind of Thursday night that turns into a long walk around the square after.

title: Bob Dylan, Lucinda Williams & Jimmie Vaughan — Chastain Park
location: Synovus Bank Amphitheater at Chastain Park, 4469 Stella Dr NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Three songwriting legends on one Chastain Park bill — Bob Dylan, Lucinda Williams, and Jimmie Vaughan. Picnic dinner + wine under the trees, and a Friday-night closer that feels like a real occasion. Premium date move.

title: Playing The Cool — Velvet Note Jazz Night
location: The Velvet Note, 4075 Old Milton Pkwy, Alpharetta, GA
description: Cool-jazz night at the Velvet Note's intimate listening room — saxophonist Mike Jones with Lil' John Roberts on drums, Joel Powell on bass, and Phil Davis on keys. A two-set Friday date with a real sit-down jazz-club feel.

title: Elements by Surya — Intimate World Music Experience
location: Abernathy Arts Center, 254 Johnson Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA
description: A intimate world-music evening at a small Sandy Springs arts center — global instrumentation, candlelit room, and the kind of date that feels like a private concert. Pair with dinner in Sandy Springs before or after.

title: 'O God My Heart is Ready' — Opening Reception at Callanwolde
location: Callanwolde Fine Arts Center, 980 Briarcliff Rd NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Opening-night reception for a juried exhibition on creation, faith, and heart — historic Callanwolde mansion, meet-the-artists, wine, and a tour of the grounds. A free, dressed-up Friday-evening date that ends with dessert in Druid Hills.
